reasoning: The informal statement asks to show that the intersection of a family of topologies on X is itself a topology on X. The Lean statement takes an arbitrary index type I, a family T : I → Set (Set X) where each T i satisfies `is_topology X (T i)`, and concludes that `⋂ i : I, T i` is a topology on X. The `is_topology` definition correctly captures the standard axioms (contains univ, closed under binary intersections, closed under arbitrary unions — empty set follows from sUnion ∅ = ∅). The indexed intersection `⋂ i : I, T i` corresponds directly to the informal $\bigcap \mathcal{T}_\alpha$. No hypotheses are added or dropped; the quantifier structure and mathematical content match exactly.
